reason: The text fragment concerns the genealogy of Isaac, his marriage to Rebekah, and the birth and childhood of Esau and Jacob. While it includes God's prophecy to Rebekah that 'two nations are in your womb' and describes their future relationship, there is no mention of any upcoming destruction—whether man-made, natural, or supernatural—nor any disaster affecting the general public. The passage is primarily narrative and genealogical, concluding with Esau selling his birthright to Jacob.
